subject: SEO Update — ARLO — 2026-07-23
html:
PageWhat ChangedTarget KeywordCheck It
/blog/what-is-an-mcp-connector (new)First foundational blog post — a plain-English explainer of what an MCP connector is, how it differs from a dashboard tool (Looker Studio) or a pipeline/API integration (Windsor.ai, Supermetrics), a client-data-safety FAQ, and a setup walkthrough matching the real 5-step OAuth flow. Internally links to all 4 live connector pages, the /compare hub, and the free-signup CTA. Discovered along the way that the blog engine itself (previously flagged empty in the original audit) was already shipped by a product commit with 4 real posts live — this is post #5."mcp connector" (KD 11, re-verified live via SE Ranking — the audit's own explicit "own this" term, previously undertargeted) + "mcp for marketing" (KD 9)askarlo.app/blog/what-is-an-mcp-connector

Needs your attention (unchanged, now 2 sessions old): the Jul 18 product commit wired real OAuth for 9 new connectors (Meta Ads, HubSpot, Salesforce, Mailchimp, MailerLite, Pipedrive, GoHighLevel, Clarity, GTM), but src/lib/connectors/catalog.ts — the file driving public status claims — still marks most of them coming_soon/waitlist with ETAs already past. Still haven't shipped a Meta Ads MCP page (or the other 8) without your call on which are actually ready for agencies today. Also noticed while reviewing comparisons.ts: /compare/whatagraph-vs-arlo and /compare/agencyanalytics-vs-arlo are both already live — further along than the 12-week plan assumed, so the comparison hub only has one real gap left (Funnel.io).

Next: two more blog posts with genuinely new angles (a GA4-specific setup walkthrough, and/or an agency-owner churn-risk piece) once catalog.ts is unblocked or on its own merits; static-rendering the marketing pages remains open and deferred to its own session; GSC submission and an SE Ranking rank-tracking project for askarlo.app both still need human access. Full detail in docs/seo-strategy/worklog.txt (top entry).
